locations.name: 巴西
locations.summary: 巴西联邦共和国（葡萄牙語：República Federativa do Brasil），通稱巴西（Brasil），是南美洲國家，位於南美東部，東臨大西洋，人口2.15億，居世界第7。其國土大部分介于赤道和南回归线之间，面積851萬平方公…

locations.name: 卡尔·林奈
locations.summary: 卡尔·冯·林奈（瑞典語：Carl von Linné；1707年5月23日—1778年1月10日），也譯為林内、林耐，原名卡尔·林奈乌斯（Carl Linnaeus），瑞典生物学家，瑞典科学院创始人兼第一任主席。林奈奠定了现代生物学命名法…

locations.name: 自然系统第十版
locations.summary: 《自然系统》第十版（英語：10th edition of Systema Naturae）是18世纪瑞典生物学家卡尔·林奈所撰写的生物学专著，于1758年和1759年分别出版上下两卷。林奈在书中为动物定义了二名法命名法则（此前他曾在175…

locations.name: 中美洲
locations.summary: 中美洲（英語：Central America，西班牙語：América central）是一個地理概念。依據不同的劃分法，屬於北美洲或南美洲的一部分，一般指連接北美洲與南美洲之間的地峽，現有七個國家（若算入墨西哥則為八個）。 範圍 範圍北…

locations.name: 南美洲
locations.summary: 南亚美利加洲（英语：South America），简称南美洲，为七大洲之一，位於西半球南部（或南半球），西臨南太平洋，西面為太平洋板塊及納斯卡板塊。太平洋板塊及納斯卡板塊之間的內營力為張力，衍生了東太平洋海嶺；而納斯卡板塊與南美洲板塊的內…

locations.name: 墨西哥
locations.summary: 墨西哥合众国（西班牙語：Estados Unidos Mexicanos），通稱墨西哥（西班牙語：México），是北美洲南部的一个主权国家，北接美国，西濱太平洋，南鄰伯利兹、危地马拉，东面墨西哥湾。其领土面积达近198万平方公里，为美洲…

locations.name: 阿根廷
locations.summary: 阿根廷共和国（西班牙語：República Argentina），通称阿根廷（西班牙語：Argentina，發音：[aɾxenˈtina] ），是位于南美洲南部的主权国家。由23个省和布宜諾斯艾利斯自治市组成的联邦共和国，占有南錐體的大部…

locations.name: 安地斯山脈
locations.summary: 安地斯山脈（西班牙語：Cordillera de los Andes；奇楚亞語：Walla Antikuna）是地球陸地上最長的山脈，位於南美洲的西岸，約7,000公里長 (或8,900公里长)，200至700公里寬（最寬的部份在南緯18…

locations.name: 厄瓜多尔
locations.summary: 厄瓜多尔共和国（西班牙語：República del Ecuador），通稱厄瓜多尔（Ecuador），是位於南美洲西北部的國家，北面與哥倫比亞相鄰，南接祕魯，西面為太平洋，另包括距離厄瓜多本土1,000公里的加拉巴哥群島。 厄瓜多於18…

locations.name: 哥伦比亚
locations.summary: 哥伦比亚共和国（西班牙語：República de Colombia），通稱哥倫比亞（西班牙語：Colombia），是一個位於南美洲西北部的国家，同時是拉丁美洲第四大國家，為南美洲國家聯盟的前成員國（2018年8月退出）。它的北部是加勒比…

locations.name: 委內瑞拉
locations.summary: 委内瑞拉玻利瓦尔共和国（西班牙語：República Bolivariana de Venezuela），通稱委內瑞拉（西班牙語：Venezuela），是一個位於南美洲北部的國家。其面積為912,050平方公里，2025年人口估計為318…

locations.name: 亞馬遜雨林
locations.summary: 亞馬遜雨林（葡萄牙語：Amazônia、西班牙語：Amazonia），又稱亞馬遜河雨林，位於南美洲亞馬遜盆地的熱帶雨林，佔地僅存約500萬平方公里，使這片雨林生機盎然的就是亞馬遜河。雨林橫越了8個國家：巴西（佔森林60%面積）、哥倫比亞、…

relations.subject: 王鷲
relations.evidence: The king vulture inhabits an estimated between southern Mexico and northern Argentina. In South America, it does not live west of the Andes, except in western Ecuador, north-western Colombia and far north-western Venezuela.{{cite book | last1 = Restall| first1 = Robin| last2 = Rodner| first2 = Clemencia| last3 = Lentino| first3 = Miguel| title = Birds of Northern South America: An Identification Guide

relations.subject: 王鷲
relations.object_id: loc-熱帶和亞熱帶濕潤闊葉林-90e2a768
relations.object: 熱帶和亞熱帶濕潤闊葉林
relations.evidence: 2| page = 68| year = 2006| publisher = Christopher Helm | isbn=0-7136-7243-9}} It primarily inhabits undisturbed tropical lowland forests as well as savannas and grasslands with these forests nearby. It is often seen near swamps or marshy places in the forests. This bird is often the most numerous or only vulture present in primary lowland forests in its range, but in the Amazon rainforest, it is typically outnumbered by the greater yellow-headed vulture, while typically outnumbered by the lesser yellow-headed, turkey, and American black vulture in more open habitats.{{cite book | last1 = Restall| first1 = Robin| last2 = Rodner| first2 = Clemencia| last3 = Lentino| first3 = Miguel| title=Birds of Northern South America: An Identification Guide

relations.subject: 王鷲

It is often seen near swamps or marshy places in the forests. This bird is often the most numerous or only vulture present in primary lowland forests in its range, but in the Amazon rainforest, it is typically outnumbered by the greater yellow-headed vulture, while typically outnumbered by the lesser yellow-headed, turkey, and American black vulture in more open habitats.{{cite book | last1 = Restall| first1 = Robin| last2 = Rodner| first2 = Clemencia| last3 = Lentino| first3 = Miguel| title=Birds of Northern South America: An Identification Guide

relations.subject: 王鷲
relations.evidence: 1 | pages=80–83 | year=2006 | publisher= Christopher Helm | isbn=0-7136-7242-0}} King vultures generally do not live above , although are found in places at altitude east of the Andes, and have been rarely recorded up to They inhabit the emergent forest level, or above the canopy. Pleistocene remains have been recovered from Buenos Aires Province in central Argentina, over south of its current range, giving rise to speculation on the habitat there at the time which had not been thought to be suitable.{{cite journal|title=First record of Sarcoramphus Dumeril 1806 (Ciconiiformes: Vulturidae) from the Pleistocene of Buenos Aires province, Argentina |author1=Noriega, Jorge I

relations.subject: 王鷲

It is often seen near swamps or marshy places in the forests. relations.subject: 王鷲
relations.evidence: However, there is evidence that suggests a decline in population, though it is not significant enough to cause it to be listed. This decline is due primarily to habitat destruction and poaching. Although distinctive, its habit of perching in tall trees and flying at high altitudes renders it difficult to monitor
relations.object_summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

relations.subject: 王鷲

relations.object_summary: 直接捕杀或非法贸易导致种群下降。
